title: "S-Video Output Problem"

forum: Multimedia Software

### Post by skipjack on 2009-05-17
So, this is really starting to bother me...  I have connected my ubuntu box "Hardy" to my JVC "27 tv via s-video.  Everything works fine if I also have my samsung monitor connected via vga at the same time.  If I disconnect the vga cable the s-video does not work anymore.  I have no idea why... here is my xorg.conf file..

```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Boardname	"ati"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	Screen		0
	Option		"TVOutFormat"	"SVIDEO"
	Option		"ConnectedMonitor"	"TV"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
EndSection

```

lspci | grep VGA output

```

01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: ATI Technologies Inc RV535 [Radeon X1650 Series] (rev 9e)
```
